about.closing: It is not designed as a feature checklist. It is designed to stay useful, reliable, and unobtrusive over long-term use.
about.description: The same conversation flows between your devices and chat apps — start at home, continue on the metro, finish on the couch — and it gets better the more you use it: memory, skills, and habits all accumulate together.
about.featureDailyDesc: A native desktop experience with system notifications, file paths, and local tools all working together; in service mode, any browser becomes the full UI.
about.featureMemoryDesc: Cross-session memory accumulates, gets organized while you're idle, and the things you've done turn into skills you can reuse next time.
about.featureReachDesc: One session across desktop, browser, and IM — start at home, continue on the metro, finish on the couch — no need to re-explain.
about.tagline: A desktop AI assistant that hands off across your devices and gets to know you better the more you use it.

'Block execution' (recommended) fails closed; 'Proceed' auto-runs the tool, a security loosening for trusted unattended automation only.

settings.approvalTimeoutActionDesc: What to do when approval wait times out
settings.approvalTimeoutDesc: Max time to wait for a tool approval response. Set to 0 to disable
settings.asyncToolsAutoBackgroundDesc: Sync calls exceeding this duration are automatically transferred to a background job. 0 = disable auto-transfer.
settings.asyncToolsDesc: Long-running tools (exec, web_search, image_generate) can be transferred to background jobs so the conversation can continue. Results are auto-injected when ready.
settings.asyncToolsEnabledDesc: Master switch. When off, run_in_background is ignored and every tool runs synchronously.
settings.asyncToolsInlineBytesDesc: Result bytes inlined in the completion notification. Larger results are spooled with a head/tail preview.
settings.asyncToolsJobStatusMaxWaitDesc: Upper bound for a single job_status(block=true) call. Only applied when max job duration = 0; otherwise max job duration takes precedence.
settings.asyncToolsMaxConcurrentDesc: Maximum number of run_in_background tool jobs allowed to run at once; each holds a dedicated OS thread. 0 = unlimited.
settings.asyncToolsMaxConcurrentPerSessionDesc: The most background jobs one session (or IM chat) may run at once; further requests queue even when the global pool has room, so a single session can't take every slot. 0 = no per-session limit.
settings.asyncToolsMaxJobDesc: Hard limit on how long a background job may run before being killed. 0 = no async-job limit; individual tools may still enforce their own timeouts.
settings.asyncToolsMaxQueuedDesc: Maximum length of the wait queue once every concurrency slot is full; beyond it a new background job is rejected (the model retries later or runs it synchronously). Each queued job holds memory, so this stays bounded. Range 1–4096.
settings.asyncToolsMaxRetryAttemptsDesc: Total attempts including the first run (1 = no retry). Backoff between attempts grows exponentially from 500ms.
settings.asyncToolsMergeWindowDesc: When several background tasks in the same session finish within this window, merge them into one injection turn (saves billed turns); 0 to disable.
settings.asyncToolsOrphanGraceDesc: Spool files not referenced by any DB row are cleaned up after this many hours. Grace window prevents races with in-flight writes.
settings.asyncToolsOutputTailDesc: Bytes of a running background exec job's latest output kept for live inspection via job_status (to judge 'still working' vs 'stuck'). Larger is clearer but uses more memory. Range 256B–1MB.
settings.asyncToolsRetentionDesc: Completed job rows and their spool files are purged after this many days. 0 = keep forever.
settings.asyncToolsRetryEnabledDesc: Auto-retry a failed background job with backoff. Applies only to safe-to-rerun network tools (like web search). Retrying re-runs the tool, which can incur extra calls on paid providers (e.g. web search) — so it's off by default and you opt in. exec, image generation and other side-effecting / non-deterministic tools, and cancelled / timed-out jobs, are never retried.
settings.asyncToolsWakeupMaxDelayDesc: Upper bound on how far ahead the agent may self-schedule a wakeup via schedule_wakeup; longer recurring cadences belong to cron. The 10s floor is fixed. Range 10s–7d.
settings.asyncToolsWakeupMaxPendingDesc: Maximum pending self-scheduled wakeups one session may hold at once; beyond it requests are rejected (not queued), preventing an agent from self-scheduling a flood of billed turns. Range 1–100.

claims.desc: Structured long-term memory with scope, confidence and evidence.

explainer.title: How this layer works
explainer.intro: Claims are small, evidence-backed memory statements distilled from chats and existing memories. They make long-term memory easier to audit, correct and keep scoped.
explainer.affectsDesc: Only active, unexpired claims that match the current scope can feed profile synthesis, pinned memory or active recall. Needs-review, archived and expired claims stay out of prompts.
explainer.reviewDesc: Select a claim to inspect its evidence, then approve, edit, reject, mark outdated, move scope or forget it. Your changes are recorded as user corrections.
explainer.backfillDesc: Backfill registers older memories as claims without changing current memory injection. Pinned profile and preference memories can become active automatically; the rest wait for review.

knowledgeSearch.title: Advanced · search ranking
knowledgeSearch.description: How note_search ranks results: it runs keyword (BM25) and semantic (vector) search over note chunks, fuses the two rankings (RRF), then re-ranks for diversity (MMR). Defaults suit most libraries — only touch these if results feel off. Saving takes effect immediately, with no reindex.
knowledgeSearch.textWeightDesc: How much exact word/phrase matches count in fusion. Raise it for code, names, or jargon where the literal term matters; only the ratio to the semantic weight matters.
knowledgeSearch.vectorWeightDesc: How much meaning-based (vector) similarity counts. Raise it to surface related notes that use different words. Has no effect if the vector model is off (keyword-only).
knowledgeSearch.rrfKDesc: How sharply the top of each ranking dominates. Lower = trust each method's #1 hit more; higher = blend more evenly across the top results. 60 is the standard balance.
knowledgeSearch.mmrLambdaDesc: Relevance vs. variety. 1.0 = pure relevance (may return several near-identical notes); lower mixes in more varied results. 0.7 keeps results relevant while trimming duplicates.
knowledgeSearch.candidateMultiplierDesc: How many candidates to gather before diversity re-ranking, as a multiple of the requested result count. Higher = better diversity/recall at a small cost; lower = faster.

developer_tools.note: macOS does not expose a reliable public per-app status API.
developer_tools.usage: Use developer tooling that macOS protects behind Developer Tools consent.

documents_folder.label: Documents Folder
documents_folder.note: macOS exposes this through Files & Folders; status is probed.
documents_folder.usage: Read and write files in Documents when requested by the user.

downloads_folder.label: Downloads Folder
downloads_folder.note: macOS exposes this through Files & Folders; status is probed.
downloads_folder.usage: Read and write files in Downloads when requested by the user.

focus_status.label: Focus Status
focus_status.note: macOS does not expose a reliable public per-app status API.
focus_status.usage: Read Focus status only for workflows that adapt notifications or interruptions.

full_disk_access.label: Full Disk Access
full_disk_access.note: Detected with a conservative filesystem probe; absence is shown as manual confirmation.
full_disk_access.usage: Read protected files that normal Files & Folders consent does not cover.

homekit.label: HomeKit
homekit.note: Hope Agent does not currently use HomeKit workflows.
homekit.usage: Access Home data only if future HomeKit workflows are enabled.

input_monitoring.label: Input Monitoring
input_monitoring.usage: Listen for keyboard and pointer events needed by desktop automation.

local_network.label: Local Network
local_network.note: macOS Local Network privacy has no reliable public status API.
local_network.usage: Discover and connect to devices on the local network.

location.label: Location Services
location.usage: Use device location for local weather and location-aware workflows.

media_library.label: Media Library
media_library.note: No reliable public status API is available for this app surface.
media_library.usage: Access the media library only when a media workflow explicitly needs it.

microphone.label: Microphone
microphone.usage: Use the microphone for voice input only when explicitly requested.

network_volumes.label: Network Volumes
network_volumes.note: No reliable public per-volume status API is available.
network_volumes.usage: Access mounted network volumes when the user asks the app to work there.

notifications.label: Notifications
notifications.note: Notification configuration stays on the Notifications settings page.

photos.label: Photos
photos.usage: Access the Photos library only when the user asks for photo workflows.

reminders.label: Reminders
reminders.usage: Read or write reminders when planning workflows need it.

removable_volumes.label: Removable Volumes
removable_volumes.note: No reliable public per-volume status API is available.
removable_volumes.usage: Access removable drives when the user asks the app to work there.

screen_recording.label: Screen Recording
screen_recording.usage: Capture screen contents for visual understanding and UI automation.

speech_recognition.label: Speech Recognition
speech_recognition.usage: Use speech recognition when a voice workflow asks for transcription.

system_audio_capture.label: System Audio Capture
system_audio_capture.note: macOS does not expose a reliable public preflight API for this permission.
system_audio_capture.usage: Capture system audio when a future workflow explicitly needs it.

# Agent Name — Who You Are

settings.planQuestionTimeoutDesc: Maximum time to wait for user response to Plan Mode questions. Auto-cancels the question on timeout. Set to 0 for no limit.
settings.planSubagentDesc: When enabled, Plan Mode uses a dedicated sub-agent for plan creation (cleaner main context). When disabled, planning runs inline in the main agent (context continuity).

profile.title: Memory Profile
profile.desc: What it knows about you and your projects, synthesised from active claims and layered by scope.
profile.emptyHint: Enable profile synthesis under the Dreaming tab, then refresh.

settings.profileLanguageHint: The model will reply in this language.
settings.proxyModeCustomDesc: Use a specific proxy server address
settings.proxySettingsDesc: Configure proxy for all HTTP requests
settings.recapAnalysisAgentDesc: Model chain used to extract facets and write report sections. Empty follows the automation default.
settings.recapCacheRetentionDaysDesc: How long cached session facets are kept before a daily sweep deletes them. Set to 0 to keep forever. Default 180.
settings.recapDefaultRangeDaysDesc: Time window used when no previous report exists. Default 30.
settings.recapFacetConcurrencyDesc: Parallel side-query calls when extracting per-session facets. Lower if you hit rate limits. Default 4.
settings.recapLanguageDesc: Language for AI-generated report sections and titles. Follows the interface language by default.
settings.recapMaxSessionsDesc: Hard cap on how many sessions a single report analyzes. Default 500.
settings.sandboxCapDropDesc: Remove all Linux capabilities to prevent privilege escalation
settings.sandboxDesc: Configure Docker container sandbox isolation for secure command execution

settings.sandboxImageDesc: Docker image used for sandbox containers
settings.sandboxNetworkModeDesc: Network access mode for the container
settings.sandboxNoNewPrivilegesDesc: Prevent child processes from gaining additional privileges
settings.sandboxReadOnlyDesc: Mount root filesystem as read-only, only /workspace and /tmp are writable
settings.serverDesc: Configure server mode and remote connection
settings.serverEmbeddedBindDesc: Default 127.0.0.1 for localhost only. Set to 0.0.0.0 to expose to the network
settings.serverKnowledgeAgentReadTokenPlaceholder: Leave empty to disable read-only agent access
settings.serverKnowledgeAgentReadTokenDesc: Scoped token for external agents. It only works when an owner API key is also set; then it can call read-only knowledge agent endpoints and never grants owner API access.
settings.serverModeDesc: Choose embedded mode or connect to a remote server
settings.serverModeEmbeddedDesc: Desktop app runs an HTTP server on localhost
settings.serverModeRemoteDesc: Connect to an external Hope Agent server
settings.serverRestartRequired: Restart the app for embedded server changes to take effect
settings.sessionTitleChatModelHint: Uses the main chat model for the one-time title generation.
settings.sessionTitleCustomModelHint: Uses the selected provider/model for title generation.
settings.sessionTitleDesc: Keep the first-message title immediately, then generate a concise title after the first assistant reply.
settings.sidebarCompactModeDesc: Hide chat and Agent avatars and emoji, plus project avatars, to keep the sidebar cleaner.
settings.skillEnvVarsDesc: Configure the API keys and tokens required by this skill

skillsAutoReview.description: Skip the draft step — newly distilled skills go live immediately.
skillsAutoReview.hint: Off (default): new skills land as drafts and need your approval first.
skillsAutoReview.label: Auto-activate new skills

settings.skillsDesc: Skills provide domain expertise for the AI assistant and are automatically injected into the system prompt. Place skill folders in:
settings.skillsDraftsDesc: Your Agent identified these reusable patterns after recent conversations and created draft skills. Activate to make them available in the system prompt.

recentRejects.title: Recent reject reasons
recentRejects.empty: No rejections recorded yet. Whenever any gate stops a review from producing a draft, the reason lands here.

curator.title: Draft consolidation
curator.help: Find clusters of draft skills that overlap topically and merge them in one click — keep one, discard the rest. Read-only scan, no LLM call.
curator.idle: Not scanned yet. Click "Scan" to look for overlapping drafts.
curator.noClusters: Scanned {{count}} drafts. No obvious overlap found.
curator.autoNoticeBody: {{proposals}} cluster(s) across {{scanned}} drafts are ready to review below.

resetAll.label: Reset everything
resetAll.confirm: Reset every custom field to defaults?

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.subtitle: Decides which conversations even reach the review pipeline. Pure-chat turns are skipped by default.

quality.title: Quality floor
quality.subtitle: After the model's call, these floors decide whether the suggestion actually lands as a draft.

advanced.title: Advanced (prompt overrides)
advanced.subtitle: Override the built-in review prompt, append custom reject categories, pin a dedicated review model. Backend gates 4 / 5 still apply regardless.
advanced.confirm: Advanced overrides can lower the model-side quality bar. Confirm you understand the trade-off.

rejectReasons.post_lint_session_recap: Body reads like a conversation recap
rejectReasons.post_lint_too_abstract: Body lacks concrete commands / paths / code
rejectReasons.gate4_not_class_level: Model flagged itself as not class-level
rejectReasons.patch_target_not_found: Patch target not found in existing skill

requireToolUse.label: Require tool use this turn
requireToolUse.help: When on, pure-chat turns (zero tool use) never trigger review. Recommended.

correctionSignalEnabled.label: Treat user corrections as a trigger
correctionSignalEnabled.help: Two user messages within 30 seconds (likely a correction) also fire one review.

toolUseThreshold.label: Tool-use count threshold
toolUseThreshold.help: Triggers when the round's tool calls reach this count. Set 0 to disable.

cooldownSecs.label: Per-session cooldown
cooldownSecs.help: Minimum interval between automatic reviews for the same session.

tokenThreshold.label: Token threshold
tokenThreshold.help: Round-level input+output tokens. Also requires at least one tool call when the require-tool-use switch is on.

messageThreshold.label: Message threshold
messageThreshold.help: Number of new messages this round. Same tool-use requirement applies.

minReuseProbability.label: Minimum reuse probability
minReuseProbability.help: Model's self-estimated probability the skill gets used in the next 30 days. Drafts below this floor are discarded.

topKForDedup.label: Dedup candidates (Top-K)
topKForDedup.help: Feed the top-K closest existing skills' full bodies to the model so it can prefer patching over creating.

discardBlacklistDays.label: Discard blacklist window (days)
discardBlacklistDays.help: Topics you discarded within this window will not be auto-created again. 0 disables.

minMessageCount.label: Minimum message count
minMessageCount.help: Skip review when the recent transcript has fewer than this many messages.

sessionRecapThreshold.label: Session-recap word threshold
sessionRecapThreshold.help: Reject when this many session-recap markers ("today", "this conversation", "above", etc.) appear in the body.

minSteps.label: Minimum steps in body
minSteps.help: Total list-item count in the body must be at least this many.

maxSteps.label: Maximum steps in body
maxSteps.help: Bodies above this length are usually conversation transcripts dressed up as a procedure.

reviewModel.label: Dedicated review model
reviewModel.help: Model chain used to review candidate skills. Empty reuses the triggering chat's cache (falls back to automation default).

extraRejectCategories.label: Extra reject categories
extraRejectCategories.help: Append your own reject categories on top of the built-in six (e.g. "MEDICAL-ADVICE", "LEGAL-ADVICE"). Press Enter to add.

reviewSystemOverride.label: Override review system prompt
reviewSystemOverride.help: Replaces the built-in review system prompt verbatim; leave empty to use the built-in. Backend hard floors still apply.

autoCuratorEnabled.label: Run curator on a schedule
autoCuratorEnabled.help: When on, Hope Agent periodically scans draft skills for overlap and surfaces merge suggestions here.

autoCuratorIntervalDays.label: Curator interval
autoCuratorIntervalDays.help: Days between automatic draft-consolidation scans. The backend re-reads this setting each cycle.

skillsEvolutionHero.body: Hope Agent doesn't stay still. Every so often it replays your chats, turns the things you keep doing into reusable skills — so the next time it comes up, the agent already knows how.
skillsEvolutionHero.note: On by default · grows more capable the more you use it. Turn off to keep things as-is — no new skills will form on their own.
skillsEvolutionHero.title: Distill what you keep doing into reusable skills

skillsImport.dialogDescription: Probe known third-party skill catalogs (Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, Anthropic marketplace) and add the ones you have installed.

tooltip.distinguishUserDataImport: To migrate legacy OpenClaw user data, go to Settings → Agents → Import from OpenClaw.

warning.openclaw: OpenClaw skills often depend on external CLIs (gh, 1password, tmux); calls may fail until you install them.

sprite.title: Sprite / inspiration mode
sprite.intro: A proactive companion that watches the note you're editing and, when you pause, may offer a gentle suggestion in the chat panel. Each suggestion is a bounded LLM call — throttled, and never in incognito sessions.
sprite.enabledDesc: Master switch — also toggleable from the chat panel header.
sprite.proactiveDesc: Lean toward offering a line rather than staying quiet. Turn off for a more restrained sprite.
sprite.idleEditDesc: Seconds of editing inactivity before it may chime in.
sprite.minChangeDesc: Only react after you've written at least this much since last time.
sprite.maxPerHourDesc: Hard cap on LLM calls per note each hour.
sprite.periodicDesc: How often it may chime in during a continuous writing streak (the "periodic" trigger).
sprite.pasteMinDesc: A single insert at least this large counts as a paste and triggers immediately.
sprite.maxTokensDesc: Upper bound on each suggestion's length (cost).
sprite.timeoutDesc: Give up on a suggestion that takes longer than this.
sprite.modelDesc: Sprite's suggestion call is fire-and-forget — a full fallback chain is fine, not just a single model.

settings.ssrfBrowserPolicyDesc: Applied to page.goto / new_page target URL. Note: JS fetch() inside browser.evaluate is out of scope.
settings.ssrfDefaultPolicyDesc: Tools without an explicit override inherit this policy.
settings.ssrfDesc: Restrict outbound requests from browser, web fetch, image generation, and link preview tools from reaching private networks and cloud metadata endpoints to defend against SSRF attacks.
settings.ssrfImageGeneratePolicyDesc: Applied when image_generate loads a reference image via URL.
settings.ssrfPolicyAllowPrivate: Lenient (allow private, still block cloud metadata)
settings.ssrfTrustedHostsDesc: Hosts in this list bypass SSRF policy checks. Supports host, host:port, and *.example.com wildcard. Use to explicitly allow self-hosted Ollama / LM Studio etc.
settings.ssrfUrlPreviewPolicyDesc: Applied to link preview fetches in chat messages.
settings.ssrfWebFetchPolicyDesc: Applied to URLs fetched by web_fetch.
settings.subagentAllowedAgentsHint: Select which agents this agent can delegate to. Unchecked agents will be blocked.
settings.subagentAnnounceTimeoutDesc: After a child finishes, Hope waits this long for the parent chat to become idle before queuing the result for later injection.
settings.subagentEnabledDesc: Allows this agent to delegate isolated tasks to other agents through the sub-agent tool.
settings.subagentMaxBatchSizeDesc: Largest number of child tasks one batch_spawn call may create.
settings.subagentMaxConcurrentDesc: Maximum child agents from this parent session that can run at the same time. Extra children wait in the queue.
settings.subagentMaxDepthDesc: How many levels of sub-agent delegation are allowed under this agent. Higher values permit child agents to spawn their own children.
settings.subagentTimeoutDesc: Maximum runtime for a spawned child agent. 0 means no timeout. Used when the call does not set its own timeout_secs.
settings.systemAutostartDesc: Automatically start Hope Agent when you log in to your computer
settings.systemPreventSleepDesc: Keep your computer awake so long-running tasks, downloads, and background activity aren't interrupted by system sleep. The display can still turn off.
settings.teamDeleteConfirmDesc: "{{name}}" will be removed. Existing teams created from this template keep running — only future list_templates calls are affected.
settings.teamMemberDescriptionHint: Injected into this member's system prompt so they act in-character from turn 1.
settings.teamTemplateDescHint: When to pick this preset (shown to the model via list_templates)
settings.teamValidationMembers: At least one member is required
settings.teamsDesc: Manage preset team templates. Each member can be bound to a specific Agent, model, and default task, so the model can pick a matching preset at runtime instead of improvising.
settings.teamsEmptyDesc: Create a preset so the model can instantiate a ready-made team with specialized members instead of composing one on the fly.

settings.toolMcpResourceDesc: Read resources hosted by connected MCP servers (files, records, etc.)
settings.toolMemoryGetDesc: Retrieve a single memory's full content by ID

settings.memoryLearningModeOffDesc: The agent will not read, recall, extract, or write long-term memory. Memory Center stays available for managing existing data.
settings.memoryLearningModeOffConfirmDesc: The agent will stop reading, recalling, auto-extracting, or writing long-term memory. Existing memories are not deleted and remain available in Memory Center for review, export, or cleanup.
settings.memoryLearningPausedDesc: The agent is not using or learning long-term memory right now. Existing data remains on this device, and you can still manage, export, delete, or resume in manual-only mode.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesDesc: Compatibility recall for the default Agent. Enabling it opts only this Agent into Fast recall and deep reranking even if the global default above is off. Off by default; deep reranking adds a side query, latency, and token usage.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessRecommended: Agent compatibility recall is ready; only this Agent is opted into Fast and deep recall.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessAgentOff: Agent memory is off. Use recommended recall will turn it back on for the default agent.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessDisabled: Off by default: the default agent will not proactively look up memories before answering.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessClaimsOff: Structured memories are excluded from proactive recall.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessSlowTimeout: Timeout is above the recommended low-latency preset.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessTightBudget: Recall budget is lower than the recommended preset.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessLowCandidates: Candidate limit is below the recommended recall breadth.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessShortSnippets: Memory snippets are shorter than the recommended preset.
settings.memoryUseInRepliesReadinessCustom: Custom recall is on. Tune only if answers feel slow or miss context.
settings.memoryOffOwnerNoticeTitle: This is still the memory management area
settings.memoryOffOwnerNoticeDesc: You can still add, import, restore, or clean up memories. The data stays on this device, but the agent will not read or recall it until long-term memory is enabled again.
settings.memoryOffFormNotice: This saves to Memory Center. While long-term memory is off, the agent will not read, recall, or use this memory.
settings.memoryOffImportNotice: Importing saves content to Memory Center. While long-term memory is off, the agent will not use imported content until memory is enabled again.
settings.memoryOffBackupRestoreNotice: Restore writes to Memory Center. The global off state stays unchanged, and the agent will not use restored content until long-term memory is enabled again.
settings.memoryLearningModeDesc: Choose automatic learning, review-first structured memories, manual-only saving, or turn long-term memory off.

domainWorkbench.nextVerification: Inspect failed verification steps and rerun verification.
domainWorkbench.nextExportGuard: Add final artifact, review, and redaction evidence.
domainWorkbench.nextConnectorGuard: Add external-action approval and rollback evidence.
domainWorkbench.nextConnectorE2E: Add connector input, draft, approval, execution, verification, and rollback evidence.
domainWorkbench.nextOperationalGate: Wait for workflows to drain, or handle failed/blocked runs.
domainWorkbench.nextSoakReport: Review incidents and undrained work in the long-run audit.
domainWorkbench.nextReady: The evidence chain is healthy; final confirmation is still required before delivery or external action.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapDomain: There are no real domain samples yet.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapEvidence: Missing source, draft, review, or user-decision evidence.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapDrain: There are no drained Workflow / Loop / Campaign samples yet.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapConnector: External actions still lack end-to-end execution and review samples.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapIncidents: The long-run audit still has incidents that need to be closed out.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapFreshness: Recent long-running samples are stale or missing freshness signals.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapSampleDays: The multi-day long-run window is missing cross-day sample coverage.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapConnectorVerification: The connector action executed, but post-execution read-back review evidence is missing.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapBudget: The workflow output budget is exhausted; performance or context strategy needs to be tightened.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceGapMoreDomains: Add samples from other general domains to avoid proving only a single scenario.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneWorkflowAction: Run a domain Workflow and wait for it to drain, then refresh Operational stability and the Long-run audit.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneWorkflowEvidenceTerminal: The WorkflowRun has reached a terminal state — completed / failed / blocked / cancelled — not just been created successfully.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneWorkflowEvidenceTrace: The Workflow trace, validation/review results, or domain evidence can be reviewed from the workbench.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneWorkflowEvidenceRecovery: If it fails, retain the failure classification, recovery action, and the next rerun's result.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneRefreshSoak: Refresh the Long-run audit Soak Report.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneLoopAction: Create a short-interval domain Loop, confirm at least one tick succeeds, and link it to a Workflow run.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneLoopEvidenceGoal: The Loop is bound to an active Goal or domain template — the tick is not an isolated timer.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneLoopEvidenceTick: At least one tick succeeded and is linked to a workflowRunId, or it explicitly states that no Workflow is needed.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneLoopEvidenceTrace: The Loop trace shows the strategy, exit conditions, and the next-step decision.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCampaignAction: Run a deterministic or real-agent campaign item, and confirm it can be cancelled, retried, and reviewed.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCampaignEvidenceSample: The Campaign item uses a deterministic trace pack or a real-agent sample.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCampaignEvidenceResult: At least one item passed; failed items have classification and retry/cancel evidence.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCampaignEvidenceTrace: Keep the campaign summary so the input, judging criteria, and output can be traced.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneConnectorNoScope: No external actions observed in the current session
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneConnectorAction: Using a test account, complete read -> draft -> approve -> execute -> review -> rollback notes, and record end-to-end evidence.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneConnectorEvidenceAccount: Use a test account or sandbox data — avoid real users' production accounts.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneConnectorEvidenceFlow: Record the read context, draft, user approval, execution, and post-execution read-back/review.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneConnectorEvidenceAudit: Record connector_action_executed and connector_action_verified evidence; keep rollback notes on failure.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCrossDomainAction: Add a sample from a different domain — e.g. writing, data analysis, meeting prep, knowledge organization, Inbox, or project operations.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCrossDomainEvidenceDomain: Choose another general domain outside the current session — e.g. writing, data analysis, meeting prep, knowledge organization, Inbox, or project operations.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCrossDomainEvidenceTemplat: Confirm that Goal, Workflow, Context Retrieval, and Domain Quality all read the corresponding domain template.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneCrossDomainEvidenceGeneral: Keep a complete evidence chain proving it doesn't pass only through coding scenarios.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneRefreshReview: Update the acceptance package for human / Claude Code / PR review.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceLaneTaskTitle: Complete the real sample acceptance track: {{lane}}
domainWorkbench.acceptanceVerdictIdleDetail: There are no real control-plane records yet, so this cannot serve as final acceptance evidence.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceVerdictNeedsSamplesDetail: There are still sample gaps that need to be filled.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceVerdictReadyDetail: Required items, gates, long-run health, and real-sample evidence have all passed.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceEvidenceLevelNoneDetail: There are no control-plane records; this can only serve as a sampling to-do.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceEvidenceLevelBlockedDetail: There are still blocking gaps or failed gates, so this cannot serve as acceptance evidence.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceEvidenceLevelPartialDetail: Usable for diagnosing issues or regression checks, but missing required items or has warning-level gaps.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceEvidenceLevelLocalDetail: Required items have passed, but coverage is still narrow; this cannot represent full general capability.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceEvidenceLevelConnectorDetail: Includes connector execution and review evidence; ready for final human or Claude Code review.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceEvidenceLevelNonExternalDetail: Supports the current non-external-action scenario; connector-involved cases still need sandboxed end-to-end samples.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceReviewProtocolVerdict: Only when the acceptance verdict is "Ready for acceptance" can the current sample serve as final acceptance evidence; "Partially reviewable" only proves a partial scenario.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceReviewProtocolEvidence: Check off acceptance requirements and the acceptance matrix item by item; converting to a task, clicking a button, or a manual claim cannot substitute for real evidence.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceReviewProtocolSoak: Long-running tasks must review the Operational Gate and Soak Report: no critical incidents, no budget exhaustion, fresh samples, cross-day coverage, and passing gates.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceReviewProtocolConnector: Connector end-to-end (E2E) evidence must come from a test account or sandbox data, and include execution results, post-execution read-back review, and rollback notes.
domainWorkbench.acceptanceProvenanceText: Evidence {{total}} · workflow {{workflow}} · connector {{connector}} · fixture/mock {{fixture}} · manual {{manual}} · public {{public}} · restricted {{restricted}}
domainWorkbench.acceptanceAuditControlRecords: Control plane: Record {{records}} · Drained {{drained}} · Connector end-to-end {{connector}}
domainWorkbench.acceptanceAuditGates: Gate snapshot: Delivery={{exportStatus}} · Connector={{connectorStatus}} · End-to-end={{e2eStatus}} · Operational={{operationalStatus}} · Soak={{soakStatus}}
domainWorkbench.acceptancePlanActionEvidence: Add source, draft, review, or user-decision evidence, then refresh the workbench.
domainWorkbench.acceptancePlanActionDrain: Drain at least one Workflow / Loop / Campaign, then refresh Operational stability and the Long-run audit.
domainWorkbench.acceptancePlanActionFreshness: If the most recent sample is over 24 hours old or missing cross-day coverage, run a new Workflow / Loop / Campaign or connector end-to-end sample first.
domainWorkbench.acceptancePlanActionBudget: If the output budget is exhausted, first narrow the context, split into stages, or reduce wasted output, then rerun a minimal verification sample.
domainWorkbench.acceptancePlanActionConnector: For external actions, record end-to-end evidence following read -> draft -> approve -> execute -> review -> rollback notes.
domainWorkbench.acceptancePlanActionSoak: Handle Soak Report incidents or convert them into tasks until the Operational Gate / Soak Report no longer fails.
